payment_note.sw: Malipo yanafanywa baada ya ukaguzi wa msimamizi na uthibitisho wa msimamizi mkuu. Njia ya malipo inakubaliwana kibinafsi.
payment_note.en: Payment is made after moderator review and admin confirmation. Payment method is agreed individually.

description.sw: Viwakilishi vyote vya nafsi — mimi/wewe/yeye/sisi/ninyi/wao — pamoja na viwakilishi vya kuonyesha na umiliki.
description.en: All personal pronouns — I/you/he/she/we/you/they — together with demonstrative and possessive pronouns.

phrases.en: Priska and I are going home
Chaha and I are going home
Chaha and Gesase are going home
Priska and Maria, are you going home?
Chaha and Gesase, are you going home?
Chaha and Priska are going home
Priska and Maria are going home
Chaha and Gesase are going home

phrases.sw: Mimi na Priska (sisi wawili tu) tunaenda nyumbani
phrases.en: Priska and I (just the two of us) are going home

phrases.sw: Mimi na Chaha (sisi wawili tu) tunaenda nyumbani
phrases.en: Chaha and I (just the two of us) are going home

phrases.sw: Chaha na Gesase (wao wawili tu) wanaenda nyumbani
phrases.en: Chaha and Gesase (just the two of them) are going home

phrases.sw: Chaha anauliza Priska na Maria (ninyi wawili tu): mtakuja?
phrases.en: Chaha says to Priska and Maria (just the two of you): will you come?

phrases.sw: Maria anasema: Chaha na Priska (wao wawili tu) wanaenda nyumbani
phrases.en: Maria says: Chaha and Priska (just the two of them) are going home

phrases.sw: Chaha anasema kwa mzee: ninyi mmefika?
phrases.en: Chaha says to the elder: have you arrived?

phrases.sw: Maria anamwona Chaha / Maria anamwona yeye
phrases.en: Maria sees Chaha / Maria sees him

phrases.sw: Maria anamwona Priska / Maria anamwona yeye
phrases.en: Maria sees Priska / Maria sees her

phrases.sw: Chaha anasema: nawaona ninyi (Maria na Priska)
phrases.en: Chaha says: I see you (Maria and Priska)

phrases.sw: Maria anasema: nawaona ninyi (Chaha na Gesase)
phrases.en: Maria says: I see you (Chaha and Gesase)

phrases.sw: Chaha anatuona sisi (sisi wawili tu — Maria na Priska)
phrases.en: Chaha sees us (just the two of us — Maria and Priska)

phrases.sw: Maria anasema: nawaona ninyi (ninyi wawili tu — Chaha na Gesase)
phrases.en: Maria says: I see you (just the two of you — Chaha and Gesase)

phrases.sw: Maria anawaona wao (wao wawili tu — Chaha na Priska)
phrases.en: Maria sees them (just the two of them — Chaha and Priska)

phrases.sw: Mzee anasema kwa Chaha: nakuona ninyi
phrases.en: The elder says to Chaha: I see you

phrases.sw: Hii ni kitabu cha Chaha / Hii ni kitabu chake
phrases.en: This is Chaha's book / This is his book

phrases.sw: Hii ni kitabu cha Priska / Hii ni kitabu chake
phrases.en: This is Priska's book / This is her book

phrases.sw: Hii ni kitabu chetu (changu na cha Priska)
phrases.en: This is our book (mine and Priska's)

phrases.sw: Hii ni kitabu chetu (cha Chaha na Gesase)
phrases.en: This is our book (Chaha's and Gesase's)

phrases.sw: Chaha anasema: hii ni kitabu chenu (cha Maria na Priska)
phrases.en: Chaha says: this is your book (Maria's and Priska's)

phrases.sw: Maria anasema: hii ni kitabu chenu (cha Chaha na Gesase)
phrases.en: Maria says: this is your book (Chaha's and Gesase's)

phrases.sw: Hii ni kitabu chao (cha Chaha na Priska)
phrases.en: This is their book (Chaha's and Priska's)

phrases.sw: Hii ni kitabu chao (cha Maria na Priska)
phrases.en: This is their book (Maria's and Priska's)

phrases.sw: Hii ni kitabu chao (cha Chaha na Gesase)
phrases.en: This is their book (Chaha's and Gesase's)

phrases.sw: Hii ni kitabu chetu (sisi wawili tu — changu na cha Priska)
phrases.en: This is our book (just the two of us — mine and Priska's)

phrases.sw: Chaha anasema: hii ni kitabu chenu (ninyi wawili tu — cha Maria na Priska)
phrases.en: Chaha says: this is your book (just the two of you — Maria's and Priska's)

phrases.sw: Hii ni kitabu chao (wao wawili tu — cha Chaha na Priska)
phrases.en: This is their book (just the two of them — Chaha's and Priska's)

phrases.sw: Mzee anasema kwa Chaha: hii ni kitabu chenu
phrases.en: The elder says to Chaha: this is your book
That man over there is going home
Whose book is that over there?
Those people over there are going home

phrases.ctx_en: (pointing at object next to me, not naming it)

phrases.en: I want that one over there
phrases.ctx_en: (pointing at object far away, not naming it)

body.sw: Katika Kiswahili na Kiingereza hakuna jinsia. Katika Kihispania na Kifaransa kuna jinsia mbili. Katika Kirusi na Kijerumani kuna jinsia tatu. Katika lugha yako ni vipi? Ukisema 'jiwe kubwa' na 'mto mkubwa' — neno 'kubwa' litakuwa sawa au tofauti?
body.en: In Swahili and English there is no gender. In Spanish and French there are two genders. In Russian and German there are three genders. What about your language? If you say 'a big stone' and 'a big river' — does the word 'big' stay the same or change?

sw: Sawa — hakuna jinsia
Tofauti — kuna jinsia → ngapi? zipi?
en: Stays the same — no gender
Changes — there is gender → how many? what are they?

if_yes.sw: Tunga mfano mmoja wa nomino ya kila jinsia na utoe tafsiri kwa Kiswahili na Kiingereza.
if_yes.en: Give one example noun for each gender and provide its translation in Swahili and English.

moderator_questions.sw: Swali 2: Upatanisho wa viwakilishi vya umiliki

body.sw: Katika Kiswahili 'kitabu chake' na 'gari lake' — neno 'wake' linabadilika kulingana na ngeli. Katika Kirusi 'его книга' na 'его машина' — neno 'его' daima ni sawa. Katika lugha yako ukisema 'jiwe lake' na 'mto wake' — neno 'wake' litakuwa sawa au tofauti?
body.en: In Swahili 'kitabu chake' and 'gari lake' — the word 'his' changes according to noun class. In Russian 'his book' and 'his car' — the word 'his' is always the same. In your language — does 'his/her' change depending on the object?

sw: Sawa
Tofauti → toa mifano kadhaa na vitu tofauti na tafsiri
en: Stays the same
Changes → give several examples with different objects and translations

moderator_questions.sw: Swali 3: Mnyambuliko wa majina (hali za nomino)

body.sw: Katika Kiswahili Chaha daima ni Chaha. Katika Kirusi: Чача — вижу Чачу — говорю Чаче. Katika lugha yako — majina na viwakilishi yanabadilika katika sentensi tofauti au daima yanabaki sawa?
body.en: In Swahili Chaha is always Chaha. In Russian: Чача — I see Чачу — I speak to Чаче. In your language — do names and pronouns change in different sentences or always stay the same?

sw: Hayabadiliki
Yanabadilika → toa mifano ya kila aina ya mabadiliko na tafsiri
en: They stay the same
They change → give examples of each type of change and translations

body.sw: Katika baadhi ya lugha kuna tofauti kati ya 'yule ninayemwona sasa hivi' na 'yule nisiyemwona lakini najua yuko.' Je, kuna tofauti kama hiyo katika lugha yako?
body.en: In some languages there is a difference between 'that one I can see right now' and 'that one I cannot see but know is there.' Is there such a difference in your language?

sw: Hapana — neno moja bila kujali uonekano
Ndiyo → toa mifano miwili (kinaonekana / hakionekani) na tafsiri
en: No — one word regardless of visibility
Yes → give two examples (visible / not visible) and translations

moderator_questions.sw: Swali 5: Idadi ya viwango vya umbali

body.sw: Tulikupa makundi matatu — karibu nami / karibu nawe / mbali nasi wote wawili. Katika lugha yako kuna viwango vitatu au ni tofauti?
body.en: We gave you three groups — close to me / close to you / far from both of us. In your language are there three levels or is it different?

sw: Viwango viwili — hii (karibu) / ile (mbali)
Viwango vitatu — kama katika sentensi zetu
Zaidi ya vitatu → toa mifano ya kila kiwango na tafsiri
en: Two levels — this (close) / that (far)
Three levels — as in our sentences
More than three → give examples for each level and translations

body.sw: Kulingana na maudhui yote — sentensi za wasemaji na majibu ya maswali — tunga kitabu cha sarufi kuhusu viwakilishi katika lugha yako.
body.en: Based on all content provided — speakers' sentences and answers to the questions — write a grammar reference on pronouns in your language.

sw: Jedwali la viwakilishi vya nafsi (nafsi zote, idadi, jinsia kama ipo)
Viwakilishi vya kitu
Viwakilishi vya umiliki — upatanisho
Viwakilishi vya kuonyesha — viwango vya umbali
Jedwali la maumbo
Mifano ya sentensi kwa kila kanuni
en: Table of personal pronouns (all persons, numbers, genders if any)
Object pronouns
Possessive pronouns — agreement
Demonstrative pronouns — distance levels
Table of forms
Example sentences for each rule

description.sw: Jinsia na ngeli za nomino, umoja na wingi, upatanisho wa kivumishi na kitenzi, uhai, hali za nomino, utambuzi.
description.en: Noun gender and classes, singular and plural, adjective and verb agreement, animacy, noun cases, definiteness.

name.sw: Nyakati za kitenzi (sasa, kale, kesho, kukanusha)

description.sw: Wakati wa sasa, wakati wa kale, wakati wa kesho, kukanusha katika nyakati zote tatu.
description.en: Present simple, past simple, future tense, negation in all three tenses.

description.sw: Upatanisho wa kitenzi na ngeli ya mtendaji, upatanisho wa nambari na ngeli, ngeli za mahali na vihusishi.
description.en: Verb agreement with subject class, number agreement with classes, locative classes and prepositions.

description.sw: Kitenzi 'kuwa' (uwepo na kutokuwepo), ujenzi wa 'nina/huna', maalum ya lugha.
description.en: Verb 'to be' (presence/absence), construction 'I have / I don't have', language specifics.

description.sw: Maswali ya ndiyo/hapana, maneno ya kuuliza: nani, nini, wapi, lini, jinsi gani, kwa nini, ngapi.
description.en: Yes/no questions, question words: who, what, where, when, how, why, how many.

description.sw: Salamu na kuaga, maneno ya heshima, nambari 1-100, siku za juma, miezi, nyakati za siku.
description.en: Greetings and farewells, polite expressions, numbers 1-100, days of the week, months, parts of the day.

phrases.sw: John anakutana na Anna mara ya kwanza: nafurahi kukujua!
phrases.en: John meets Anna for the first time: nice to meet you!
Anna responds to John: nice to meet you too!
John says goodbye to the elder
Anna says thank you, John responds: ___
Excuse me, where is the road?

phrases.sw: Moja, mbili, tatu, nne, tano, sita, saba, nane, tisa, kumi
phrases.en: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten

phrases.sw: Kumi, ishirini, thelathini, arobaini, hamsini, sitini, sabini, themanini, tisini, mia moja
phrases.en: Ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, sixty, seventy, eighty, ninety, one hundred

phrases.sw: Kumi na moja, kumi na mbili, ishirini na moja, ishirini na mbili, hamsini na tano
phrases.en: Eleven, twelve, twenty one, twenty two, fifty five

phrases.sw: Wa kwanza, wa pili, wa tatu, wa nne, wa tano, wa sita, wa saba, wa nane, wa tisa, wa kumi
phrases.en: First, second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, seventh, eighth, ninth, tenth
John lives in the first house

phrases.sw: Jumatatu, Jumanne, Jumatano, Alhamisi, Ijumaa, Jumamosi, Jumapili
phrases.en: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday

phrases.sw: Januari, Februari, Machi, Aprili, Mei, Juni, Julai, Agosti, Septemba, Oktoba, Novemba, Desemba
phrases.en: January, February, March, April, May, June, July, August, September, October, November, December
John will come in the evening

phrases.sw: Jana, leo, kesho, sasa, baadaye, hivi karibuni
phrases.en: Yesterday, today, tomorrow, now, later, soon
Tomorrow we are going to the market

phrases.sw: Sasa ni saa tisa na nusu
Sasa ni saa nne na dakika ishirini
John atakuja saa moja na dakika thelathini
phrases.en: John will come at seven thirty

body.sw: Katika Kiswahili 'watu wawili' lakini 'vitabu viwili' — neno 'wawili/viwili' linabadilika kulingana na ngeli. Katika Kiingereza 'two' haibadiliki kamwe. Katika lugha yako — nambari inabadilika kulingana na kitu au daima ni sawa?
body.en: In Swahili 'watu wawili' (two people) but 'vitabu viwili' (two books) — the word 'two' changes according to noun class. In English 'two' never changes. In your language — does the number change depending on the object or always stay the same?

sw: Ni sawa — nambari haibadiliki
Inabadilika → toa mifano na vitu tofauti na tafsiri
en: Always the same — numbers do not change
Changes → give examples with different objects and translations

body.sw: Katika baadhi ya lugha nambari 1 na 2 zina maumbo maalum — zinapatana na nomino tofauti na nambari nyingine. Kwa mfano katika Kirusi nambari 1 na 2 zinabadilika kulingana na jinsia. Katika lugha yako — nambari 1 na 2 zina maalum au zinafanya kazi kama nyingine zote?
body.en: In some languages numbers 1 and 2 have special forms — they agree with nouns differently from other numbers. In Russian numbers 1 and 2 change by gender. In your language — do numbers 1 and 2 have special forms or work like all other numbers?

sw: Zinafanya kazi kama nyingine zote
Zina maalum → toa mifano na tafsiri
en: Same as all other numbers
Special forms exist → give examples and translations

body.sw: Kulingana na maudhui yote — maneno, misemo ya wasemaji na majibu ya maswali — tunga kitabu kifupi cha sarufi kuhusu mada hii katika lugha yako.
body.en: Based on all content provided — words, speakers' phrases and answers to the questions — write a short grammar reference on this topic in your language.

sw: Salamu — orodha kamili na maelezo ni lini kutumia
Kuaga — orodha kamili
Maneno ya heshima na majibu yake
Nambari — je, zinapatana na ngeli, maumbo maalum ya 1 na 2
Siku za juma — maneno yake au ya mpangilio
Miezi — majina yake au ya kukopa
Jinsi ya kusema wakati — saa, dakika, nyakati za siku
en: Greetings — full list with notes on when to use each
Farewells — full list
Polite expressions and responses
Numbers — agreement with classes, special forms for 1 and 2
Days of the week — own words or ordinal
Months — own names or borrowed
How to express time — hours, minutes, parts of the day

description.sw: Familia na ndugu, sehemu za mwili, kazi za msingi.
description.en: Family and relatives, body parts, basic professions.

description.sw: Magonjwa na hali ya afya, hisia na hali za kihisia.
description.en: Illnesses and health conditions, emotions and emotional states.

description.sw: Nyumba na sehemu zake, vitu vya nyumbani, nguo, chakula na vinywaji.
description.en: House and its parts, household items, clothing, food and drinks.

description.sw: Hali ya hewa, wanyama wa nyumbani na wa mwituni, asili, kijiji na mji.
description.en: Weather, domestic and wild animals, nature, village and town.

description.sw: Vitenzi vya msingi (~40-50), vivumishi vya msingi (~20-30), rangi.
